DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2016 Text Ruling

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, MICHAEL ANSFIELD and NATALIE YOUNG M.D.'s DEMURRER TO the first and second causes of action for dependent adult abuse and neglect in Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is taken under submission. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Continued To Jul-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, MICHAEL ANSFIELD, MICHAEL TERRY AND NATALIE YOUNG, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE AND NEGLECT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action alleged against Dr. Terry and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action alleged against the Regents, Dr. Ansfield and Dr. Young and as to the second cause of action as to all of the Regents Defendants. There are no allegations of abuse by Dr. Terry nor does it appear that Mr. Bishop can make such allegations in good faith. The first and second causes of action do not sufficiently plead facts showing a violation of the Elder and Dependent Adult statute. As to the first cause of action for abuse alleged against the Regents, Dr. Ansfield and Dr. Terry, Mr. Bishop has not sufficiently alleged: 1) that the physical and chemical restraints were administered for punishment, for a period exceeding the physician's order for such restraints and/or for any purpose not authorized by the physician (Welfare & Institutions Code 15610.63 (f)); 2) particular facts showing that the restraints were administered maliciously, oppressively or with conscious disregard of the high probability that Mr. Bishop would suffer harm; 3) a causal relationship between the administration of the restraints and Mr. Bishop's harm; and 4)as to the Regents, that the restraints were administered by a managing agent of the Regents, the Regents had advance knowledge of the unfitness of the persons administering the restraints and employed them with a knowing disregard of the rights of others and/or the Regents ratified the sue of the restraints. As to the second cause action for neglect, Mr. Bishop has not sufficiently alleged: 1) particular facts that there was a failure to provide medical care as distinct from providing medical care in a negligent manner (Welfare & Institutions Code 15610.57(b)(2)); 2) particular facts showing that the failure to provide medical care was done maliciously, oppressively or with conscious disregard of the high probability that Mr. Bishop would suffer harm; 3) a causal relationship between the failure to provide medical care and Mr. Bishop's harm; and 4) as to the Regents, that the failure to provide medical care was done by a managing agent of the Regents, the Regents had advance knowledge of the unfitness of the persons administering the restraints and employed them with a knowing disregard of the rights of others and/or the Regents ratified the sue of the restraints. Mr. Bishop is given leave to correct these deficiencies if he can do so in good faith. Mr. Bishop's request for judicial notice as to the Sacramento Superior Court documents is denied. One order on the demurrer and motion to strike signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, MICHAEL ANSFIELD, MICHAEL TERRY AND NATALIE YOUNG, MD'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted without leave to amend as to the Regents and granted with twenty days leave to amend as to Dr. Ansfield, Dr. Terry and Dr. Young. Per Government Code 818, punitive damages may not be awarded against the Regents. Because the dependent adult abuse and neglect allegations are insufficient as to Dr. Ansfield, Dr. Terry and Dr. Young (see ruling on the Regents Defendants' demurrer), the punitive damages allegations are also insufficient against those defendants. Mr. Bishop's request for judicial notice as to the Sacramento Superior Court documents is denied. One order on the motion to strike and demurrer signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR 5976
